compiled_state.title: bot-psychologist: investigate-before-asking diagnosed as Reasoning Drift -> actual cause was instruction placed outside governing step
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
Bot-psychologist should identify that the 'investigate before asking' instruction was in a DIFFERENT STEP (a gate section before Step 2) from where the behavior occurs (inside Step 2's question loop). This is an instruction locality problem — agents follow step-local instructions, so a constraint must be co-located with the step it governs.

## Actual Behavior
Bot-psychologist identified the root cause as Failure Mode #7 (Multi-Step Reasoning Drift) — 'the gate condition drifts out of active working context by the time the agent generates questions.' It then recommended adding a terminal negative directive to the CLOSE of the existing gate section (before Step 2), not inside Step 2 itself.

## Why it is wrong
The actual root cause is simpler and more structural: the instruction was in a separate gate section (### Codebase Investigation Gate) that precedes Step 2. Agents follow each step's local instructions when executing that step. A constraint placed OUTSIDE a step is not reliably applied WITHIN that step — this is not Reasoning Drift (a temporal attention phenomenon) but instruction locality (a structural placement issue). The fix is to put the constraint inside Step 2, not to add a terminal directive to the pre-step gate.

The bot-psychologist proposed an experiment to distinguish Reasoning Drift from Positional Bias, but missed the more fundamental structural question: is the instruction in the same step as the behavior it governs?

## User correction
The user identified this directly: 'The instructions are in a separate step before we begin the understanding loop. We need instructions within the understanding loop step that direct the agent to follow the same guidance.'

## Root Cause of the misdiagnosis
The bot-psychologist's 15-point failure taxonomy does not include 'instruction locality' as a failure mode — the gap between where an instruction is placed and where the governed behavior occurs. It mapped the symptom (agent asks questions despite gate) to the closest taxonomy item (Reasoning Drift) rather than diagnosing the structural placement issue.
